insight: For Spain, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a neutral impact on their momentum, with the team gaining momentum in only half of the breaks, resulting in a mean change of zero. When Spain was pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their momentum in only one out of two instances, with the average change in momentum also being zero. This suggests that the breaks have not significantly disrupted Spain's flow, but also haven't provided a notable boost. The fact that there were no instances where Spain was not pressing before a break means that it's unclear how the team would respond in such a situation, but overall, the breaks seem to have had a minimal effect on Spain's performance.
